title: Update onboarding asset installation and scaffold messaging for split skill roots
description: Adjust onboarding asset installers and scaffold flow so initialized projects receive subagent Codex skill templates under `templates/skills/`, keep operator exceptions under `.agents/skills/`, and report/stage the new paths correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Onboarding installs subagent Codex skill templates into `templates/skills/` instead of `.agents/skills/`.
Onboarding still installs or preserves operator-facing exceptions under `.agents/skills/` without moving `spec-management`.
Scaffold messages and staged git paths reflect the split model rather than describing `.agents/skills/` as the full Codex skill catalog.

handoff_summary.completed:
- Added `install_skill_templates()` to `src/agent_takt/onboarding/assets.py` that copies from `packaged_skill_templates_dir()` into `project_root/templates/skills/`.
- Updated `install_agents_skills()` docstring to clarify it installs operator exceptions (memory + spec-management), not the full subagent skill catalog.
- Updated `scaffold_project()` in `scaffold.py` to call `install_skill_templates()` in step 4 with conditional success/warn messaging, updated `.agents/skills/` message from "catalog" to "operator exceptions", and included `written_skill_templates` in `all_installed`.
- Exported `install_skill_templates` from `__init__.py` (both import and `__all__`).
- Syntax verified with `python -m py_compile` on all three modified files.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. The new `install_skill_templates()` call in `scaffold_project()` uses the real bundled `_data/templates/skills/` data (no fake in the existing `TestScaffoldProject._make_fake_dirs()` test). The real data directory exists, so those tests will copy live templates instead of fakes — which is correct behaviour for verifying the split model. Tests that check `.agents/skills/` contents are unaffected.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester bead to verify scaffold installs templates/skills/, .agents/skills/ operator exceptions are preserved, and scaffold output messages correctly describe the split.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — changes are additive. `upgrade.py` is not modified; `templates/skills/` files will be passed to `write_assets_manifest()` but filtered out by `_BUNDLED_ASSET_PREFIXES` (which does not include `templates/skills/`), meaning those files won't be tracked in the upgrade manifest until a follow-up bead updates `upgrade.py`.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Added `install_skill_templates()` as a thin wrapper around `copy_asset_dir()` pointing at `packaged_skill_templates_dir()`, consistent with the existing pattern for `install_agents_skills()` and `install_claude_skills()`. The function is placed between `install_templates()` and `install_agents_skills()` to reflect the logical order (agent guardrails → subagent skill templates → operator exceptions → Claude catalog). The `commit_scaffold()` stage_paths `"templates/"` already covers `templates/skills/` so no change was needed there. The `__init__.py` export was added to keep the public API surface consistent.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `install_skill_templates()` copies files from `_data/templates/skills/` into `project_root/templates/skills/`; (2) `scaffold_project()` creates `templates/skills/` and populates it with subagent skill files; (3) `.agents/skills/` still contains only the operator exceptions (memory + spec-management) after scaffold; (4) the scaffold output messages include "subagent skill template(s) into templates/skills/" and "operator exceptions"; (5) overwrite=False skips existing `templates/skills/` files and emits a warning; (6) `install_skill_templates()` exported from `agent_takt.onboarding` namespace.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: `upgrade.py`'s `_BUNDLED_ASSET_PREFIXES` and `_compute_bundled_catalog()` do not yet include `templates/skills/`, so `takt upgrade` will not track or update these files. The files are passed into `write_assets_manifest()` during init but will be silently filtered out. A follow-up bead should update `upgrade.py` to add `templates/skills/` to the tracked prefixes and bundled catalog enumeration.

conflict_risks: None — changes are additive. `upgrade.py` is not modified; `templates/skills/` files will be passed to `write_assets_manifest()` but filtered out by `_BUNDLED_ASSET_PREFIXES` (which does not include `templates/skills/`), meaning those files won't be tracked in the upgrade manifest until a follow-up bead updates `upgrade.py`.

execution_history.summary: Added `install_skill_templates()` to `assets.py` that installs bundled subagent Codex skill templates from `_data/templates/skills/` into `project_root/templates/skills/`. Updated `scaffold_project()` in `scaffold.py` to call it (step 4), report the split via distinct console messages (`templates/skills/` for subagent templates, `.agents/skills/` described as "operator exceptions"), and include the written files in the manifest's `all_installed` list. Exported the new function from `__init__.py`. The `commit_scaffold()` `stage_paths` already uses `"templates/"` which covers both `templates/agents/` and `templates/skills/`, so no change was needed there.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
